/-- If the co-domain `β` of an injective function `f : α → β` has decidable equality, then
the domain `α` also has decidable equality. -/
def 
injective.decidable_eq: {α : Sort u_1} → {β : Sort u_2} → {f : α → β} → [inst : DecidableEq β] → injective f → DecidableEq α

injective 
f: α → β
f) : 
DecidableEq: Sort u_1 → Sort (max 1 u_1)
DecidableEq 
α: Sort u_1
α :=
λ _ _ => 
decidable_of_iff: {b : Prop} → (a : Prop) → (a ↔ b) → [inst : Decidable a] → Decidable b
decidable_of_iff

_

I.

/-- `g` is a partial inverse to `f` (an injective but not necessarily
  surjective function) if `g y = some x` implies `f x = y`, and `g y = none`

  and a right inverse if `f` is surjective). -/

/-- On non-dependent functions, `function.update` can be expressed as an `ite` -/

update_apply: ∀ {β : Sort u_1} (f : α → β) (a' : α) (b : β) (a : α), update f a' b a = if a = a' then b else f a

a, b: α

v: β a

w: β b

f: (a : α) → β a

c: α

h
(if h : c = b then (_ : b = c) ▸ w else if h : c = a then (_ : a = c) ▸ v else f c) =
  if h : c = a then (_ : a = c) ▸ v else if h : c = b then (_ : b = c) ▸ w else f c

pos
(if h : c = b then (_ : b = c) ▸ w else if h : c = a then (_ : a = c) ▸ v else f c) =
  if h : c = a then (_ : a = c) ▸ v else if h : c = b then (_ : b = c) ▸ w else f c

β✝: α✝ → Sort v

α': Sort w

inst✝²: DecidableEq α✝

inst✝¹: DecidableEq α'

  if h : c = a then (_ : a = c) ▸ v else if h : c = b then (_ : b = c) ▸ w else f c

  if h : c = a then (_ : a = c) ▸ v else if h : c = b then (_ : b = c) ▸ w else f c

/-- `extend f g e'` extends a function `g : α → γ`
along a function `f : α → β` to a function `β → γ`,
by using the values of `g` on the range of `f`
and the values of an auxiliary function `e' : β → γ` elsewhere.

Mostly useful when `f` is injective. -/

extend: (α → β) → (α → γ) → (β → γ) → β → γ

e' : 
β: Type u_2
β → 
γ: Type u_3
γ) : 
β: Type u_2
β → 
γ: Type u_3
γ :=
λ

b => if 
h: ¬∃ a, f a = b
h : ∃ 
a: α
a, 
f: α → β
f

a = 
b: β
b then

g (
Classical.choose: {α : Type u_1} → {p : α → Prop} → (∃ x, p x) → α
Classical.choose 
h: ∃ a, f a = b

section bicomp
variable {
α: Type u_1
α 
β: Type u_2

/-- Compose a binary function `f` with a pair of unary functions `g` and `h`.
If both arguments of `f` have the same type and `g = h`, then `bicompl f g g = f on g`. -/
def 
bicompl: (γ → δ → ε) → (α → γ) → (β → δ) → α → β → ε
bicompl (
f: γ → δ → ε
f : 
γ: Type u_3
γ → 
δ: Type u_4

ε: Type u_5
ε) (

g :

γ: Type u_3
γ) (
h: β → δ
h : 
β: Type u_2
β → 
δ: Type u_4
δ) (
a: α
a 
b: β
b) :=

f: γ → δ → ε
f (
g: α → γ
g 
a: α
a) (
h: β → δ
h 
b: β
b)

/-- Compose an unary function `f` with a binary function `g`. -/

f: γ → δ

b: β

a: α
a

b)

lemma 
uncurry_bicompl: ∀ {α : Type u_1} {β : Type u_2} {γ : Type u_4} {δ : Type u_5} {ε : Type u_3} (f : γ → δ → ε) (g : α → γ) (h : β → δ),
  uncurry (bicompl f g h) = uncurry f ∘ Prod.map g h

f: γ → δ → ε
f : 
γ: Type u_4

δ: Type u_5
δ →

section uncurry

/-- Records a way to turn an element of `α` into a function from `β` to `γ`. The most generic use
is to recursively uncurry. For instance `f : α → β → γ → δ` will be turned into
`↿f : α × β × γ → δ`. One can also add instances for bundled maps. -/
class 
HasUncurry: Type u → outParam (Type v) → outParam (Type w) → Type (max (max u v) w)

α : 
Type u: Type (u + 1)
Type u) (
β: outParam (Type v)

outParam: Type (v + 1) → Type (v + 1)

Type v: Type (v + 1)
Type v)) (
γ: outParam (Type w)
γ : 
outParam: Type (w + 1) → Type (w + 1)
outParam (
Type w: Type (w + 1)
Type w)) where
  
uncurry: {α : Type u} → {β : Type v} → {γ : Type w} → [self : HasUncurry α β γ] → α → β → γ
uncurry : 
α: Type u
α → (
β: outParam (Type v)
β → 
γ: outParam (Type w)
γ)

/-- A set of functions "separates points"
